methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $539M - $5.3B.

data.narrative:
Recursion Pharmaceuticals is a publicly listed biotech company currently valued at $1.6B after raising $2B+. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 11 comparable companies across Biotech & Health—3 public (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private entities spanning Series B through public stages, including Abridge ($5.3B), Chai Discovery ($3.8B), New Limit ($3.1B), Freenome ($1.1B), and Alto Neuroscience ($1.0B).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach. Across six same-sector peers filtered for similar valuation tier, the median valuation is $2.1B, with a range spanning $539M to $5.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied, as Recursion's revenue is not disclosed in available data—a limitation typical for private or early-stage public biotech companies, preventing calculation against the sector's 6.9x price-to-sales median observed across three public comparables.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.8B (low) to $2.4B (high), centered on $2.1B. Recursion's current $1.6B valuation sits below this range, reflecting either market discount relative to peers or potential methodological constraints from single-method triangulation. Key uncertainties include undisclosed revenue metrics, the heterogeneity within the peer set (ranging from Series B to public), and stage-specific performance variation unaccounted for in median-based approaches.
